### Post by John.Michael.Kane on 2005-10-13
This issue has already been posted. at this time there is no fix, and it has been post to bugzilla. you can post your issueswith yourtouch pad there aswell.

---

### Post by Pausanias on 2005-10-13
I and many others had the same problem during Breezy's development. The issue is with the "regressed" Xorg driver in Breezy. Check out this thread:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=60494](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=60494)

Who says there is now fix? Basically, the solution is to issue the following command, type in your password, and then reboot:

```

sudo sh -c "echo options psmouse proto=exps > /etc/modprobe.d/psmouse.modprobe"

```

That fixed everything for me.

Other, more complicated solutions involving recompilation of the driver can be read up on in that thread above.

---

### Post by John.Michael.Kane on 2005-10-14

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=60494](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=60494)

Who says there is now fix? Basically, the solution is to issue the following command, type in your password, and then reboot:

```

sudo sh -c "echo options psmouse proto=exps > /etc/modprobe.d/psmouse.modprobe"

```

That fixed everything for me.

Other, more complicated solutions involving recompilation of the driver can be read up on in that thread above.[/QUOTE]


Thanks Pausanias.  That at least gets me going on my Inspiron 8200.  Happy again.

### Post by Cozon on 2005-10-14
Same thing here, my ALPS touchpad worked with Hoary, and doesn't with Breezy. It is a Toshiba Satellite A10-S403D.
Adding "options psmouse proto=exps" to modprobe.d prevents Xorg from starting because synaptics module does not find the touchpad :
```
(II) Synaptics touchpad driver version 0.13.6
touchpad no synaptics event device found (checked 2 nodes)
...
Query no Synaptics: 6003C8
(EE) touchpad no synaptics touchpad detected and no repeater device
(EE) touchpad Unable to query/initialize Synaptics hardware.
(EE) PreInit failed for input device "touchpad"
(II) UnloadModule: "synaptics"
(WW) No core pointer registered

```

Since it works with FC4, I replaced breezy's synaptics_drv.o with FC4 one and loaded psmouse with proto=any ... and it works :cool: 

So, if the previous method doesn't work for you, try this :
```
sudo sh -c "echo options psmouse proto=any > /etc/modprobe.d/psmouse.modprobe"
```
and reboot
If it still doesn't work, download [ATTACH]2898[/ATTACH], gunzip and save it to /usr/X11R6/lib/modules/input/synaptics_drv.o and restart X
